task_completion.description: Open the low-contrast image in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP application is open and displaying a flat, washed-out, low-contrast image on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Colors menu in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Colors' menu is clicked and expanded in the top menu bar of GIMP.

task_completion.description: Apply the Auto Stretch Contrast feature
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Auto' submenu is expanded and 'Stretch Contrast' is selected, AND/OR the image on the canvas visibly updates to show improved tonal range (darker blacks and brighter whites).

task_completion.description: Initiate the export process to save the modified image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' or 'Save As' dialog box is open in GIMP.
The file name is set to 'output.png' and the destination folder is set to 'Documents' (path /home/ga/Documents/). The 'Export' or 'Save' button is clicked.

integrity.description: Agent used the GIMP GUI rather than terminal commands to modify the image
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GIMP graphical interface (menus, canvas, dialogs) rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick's 'convert'.

integrity.description: Agent actually applied the contrast stretch rather than downloading a pre-made image
integrity.visual_evidence: The image contrast visibly changes on the GIMP canvas during the trajectory, proving the modification was done within the editor.

integrity.description: Agent exported the file as a proper PNG
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog specifically shows PNG export settings (like compression level or interlacing) after clicking Export, ensuring it wasn't just saved as an XCF or JPEG with a wrong extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task expects the agent to use GIMP's built-in 'Colors -> Auto -> Stretch Contrast' feature. The original image is an 800x533 JPEG with very low contrast (washed out, standard deviation ~26.6). You should look for visual confirmation that the image's contrast improves (blacks become darker, whites become brighter) on the canvas before it is exported to the exact path '/home/ga/Documents/output.png'.
